bullets: Controlled experiment using no-limit Texas Hold'em, with frontier language models playing homogeneous self-play and heterogeneous mixed-model games; each model's decisions are the unit of observation.

Unnamed frontier LLMs play poker while behaviour is quantified by participation, voluntary engagement in uncertain hands, and proactiveness, pre-flop risk escalation, with no comparison to a human or ground-truth benchmark.

Models display stable, model-specific risk profiles spanning conservative to aggressive; profiles stay largely robust as opponents change, while under risk pressure adaptation ranges from broad contraction to near-invariant behaviour.
Industrial equipment maintenance support; the authors build a hybrid system that answers operator questions from a maintenance database, with sample, period, and geography not stated.
An unnamed large language model is combined with text-to-SQL and semantic pre-retrieval to turn queries into SQL and pull historical remedies, evaluated through experiments and an ablation study.

Grounding answers in database records improves traceability, and the ablation shows semantic pre-retrieval supplies vocabulary bridging under input noise that prompt engineering alone cannot replicate deterministically.
validation_note: experiments and ablation, no accuracy figure reported

bullets: Conceptual essay with no dataset, synthesizing mechanistic interpretability research and labour-economics studies to reframe how large language models should be understood.
The authors use no model themselves; they argue LLMs are best seen as a compressed, queryable database of human knowledge, written in training and read at inference.

Drawing on labour-economics evidence, the essay claims LLMs function as knowledge distribution technologies that close skill gaps where missing knowledge binds while leaving judgment scarce, foregrounding governance questions.
Panel of 606 foundational open-weight large language models released on Hugging Face between July 2024 and December 2025, with downloads tracked over time as the adoption measure.
No model is used as a tool; the models are the object, and the authors regress downloads on contributor prior success and model novelty, including separate monthly snapshot regressions.

Prior success strongly predicts adoption and novelty has a positive effect; the novelty payoff is far larger for contributors without a track record, though established contributors lead at every snapshot.
Economic model of LLM serving under shared compute, with GPT-2 experiments on linear-regression tasks and a review of documentation from 21 major AI platforms spanning 2021 to 2025.
The paper derives when users should choose supervised fine-tuning versus in-context learning under congestion; GPT-2 experiments validate the theory's predictions rather than measuring any document.
SFT and ICL dominate in different regimes and congestion can flip the ranking; offering both never lowers platform profit, and the share of platforms offering both rose from 9.5 to 71.4 percent.
A 26-week supply-chain replenishment benchmark built as a factored partially observable Markov decision process with six hidden factor processes, run over fifty seeds with curated stress profiles.
Claude Sonnet 5, GPT-5.4, DeepSeek-V4-Pro and Grok 4.5 act as replenishment agents, scored between a symptom-blind floor and an exact per-factor Bayes-filter oracle on the same observation stream.

Models detect 84 to 88 percent of hidden failures, usually within a week, yet skill scores span 0.62 to -0.23, with two of four below the symptom-blind floor and 34 to 43 percent of diagnosed weeks ending in stockout.
Matched panel of 30 Japanese Prime Market firms across three reporting years, FY2022 to FY2024, covering 60 targeted prior-year firm-year cells of ESG narrative disclosure.
A four-model consensus of claude-opus-4-7, gpt-5.5, gemini-3.1-pro and deepseek-v4-pro scores ESG narratives, with reliability judged by inter-model dispersion and no human coding as ground truth.

Only 48 percent of cells reached full comparability and 72.4 percent of 87 change observations were stable within model dispersion; narrative comparability does not imply longitudinal identifiability.
validation_note: inter-model dispersion only, no human ground truth
